title: The Betting Ring
desc: Colosseum night. The odds are terrible. The crowd is worse.

steps.text:
Fight night at the UnderWorld Colosseum. The stands are packed, the air is thick with smoke and shouting, and the betting runners are weaving through the crowd like fish in a current.

A runner shoves a slate into your hands. "Hold this. Count the wagers. I'll be back."

The slate is covered in scratched numbers — names, amounts, odds. The betting system in the UnderWorld isn't regulated. It's barely a system. It's more of a shouting match with currency.

Two creatures approach simultaneously, both wanting to bet on different fighters.

options.label: Take the bet on Krekk — the crowd favorite
Take the bet on the unknown newcomer

steps.text:
The Krekk bet is safe money. Everyone's betting on Krekk. The odds are terrible — two-to-one at best. But the bettor slaps three fire-opals on your slate and grins.

The fight starts. Krekk is big, experienced, confident. The newcomer is fast but small. For two minutes, Krekk dominates — heavy strikes, aggressive movement. The crowd loves it.

Then the newcomer does something unexpected. Nobody's quite sure what — it happens too fast. One moment Krekk is swinging. The next, Krekk is on the ground and the newcomer is standing over him, breathing hard.

The crowd goes silent. Then erupts. Half in fury, half in disbelief.

The runner returns, looks at your slate, looks at the arena. "Krekk lost?" She starts calculating payouts. The bettor who put three fire-opals on Krekk stares at the arena with the hollow expression of a creature watching their savings evaporate.

"That's the Colosseum," the runner says. "Nobody wins forever."

options.label: Hand back the slate and get out before the crowd turns ugly

steps.text:
The newcomer bet is risky. Nobody knows this fighter — no record, no reputation. The odds are twelve-to-one. The bettor puts down a single fire-opal and shrugs. "Gut feeling."

The fight is brutal. Krekk is the favorite for a reason — powerful, technical, ruthless. The newcomer takes hits. Lots of hits. The crowd counts them like a sport within the sport.

But the newcomer keeps getting up. And each time, the newcomer is faster. Adapting. Learning Krekk's patterns. In the final exchange, the newcomer slips inside Krekk's guard and lands something definitive.

Krekk goes down. The arena detonates with noise.

Your bettor pushes through the crowd, grinning like a maniac. "Twelve-to-one! I knew it!" The runner stares at the payout calculation on the slate. It's a lot of fire-opals.

"Gut feeling," the bettor says again, collecting twelve opals from the pile. The runner watches them go with the pained expression of someone whose gut feels nothing.

options.label: Return the slate, decline a career in gambling
